## Routing

Every substantive request in this repo routes through the team orchestrator: invoke `Skill(skill: "{{TEAM_SKILL}}")` and let it run the podcast pipeline (format selection → script → GATE 1 review → voice → GATE 2 listening review → master → show notes → publish). Do not call specialist agents directly unless the user explicitly names one.

The agents and skills under `.claude/` are **generated** from `{{AA_PATH}}` — never edit them here. To change an agent: edit its source in the factory, then run `aa sync {{TARGET_PATH}}`.
<!-- AA:ROUTING:END -->

## Project layout

```
research/       source report / story brief, citations
script/         script.md (speaker-tagged, segment-marked)
assets/audio/   seg-NN-*.wav, episode-vo.mp3, durations.json
publish/        mastered episode.mp3, chapters, show-notes.md, RSS item, promo drafts
project.json    format decision + rationale + phase gates status
```

## Production rules (locked preferences)

- **Voice:** Nick's cloned voice "Nick 2" via ElevenLabs. VOICE_ID lives in Nick's ElevenLabs account, never committed. If unset, STOP and ask — never fall back to a stock voice silently.
- **Audio review:** per-segment WAVs (surgical re-record) AND one concatenated review MP3.
- **Format:** pick what serves the source (solo essay / two-host / host+guest) — never default to two-host because NotebookLM does.
- **Mastering target:** -16 LUFS, chapter marks.
- **Wiki first:** authoritative knowledge base is `~/Workspaces/wiki/`.

## Parallel Agent Git Rules (CRITICAL)

- **ONLY commit files YOU changed in THIS session** — always `git add <specific-paths>`, NEVER `git add -A` or `git add .`
- NEVER: `git reset --hard`, `git checkout .` / `git restore .`, `git clean -fd`, `git stash`, `git commit --no-verify`, force-push to main
- Large audio intermediates (takes, backups) stay untracked by default — see `.gitignore`
